title: Hieracium marchettii
taxonomy: Hieracium marchettii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as having composite flowers and a taproot system. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium marchettii is more specifically classified within the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is further classified within the subfamily Cichorioideae. This classification is a part of the overall taxonomy of Hieracium marchettii.

intro: Hieracium marchettii is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in relieving digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium marchettii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Hieracium marchettii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being incorporated into capsules and infusions, to take advantage of its health benefits.
